spec.purpose: Wave 3 part 2: build the Orchestrator layer — typed workflow API that wraps the Store with safety/audit/economy. These are the entry points the MCP server will eventually call. Spec 157+171 (W3-001..W3-005) made the Store trustworthy; this wave makes it usable.
spec.exit_criteria: 5 orchestrators (O1-O5) shipped, each with tests, drift_log, and persistence. Orchestrator struct + types + LLM interface all landed. Spec 172 closes.
decisions: LLMClient is an interface with a MockClient for tests; production client (dark-scrapper integration) deferred to Wave 4+
ResearchBackend is an interface; mock backend for tests; real backends plug in later
Each orchestrator method: validate input → apply safety (canary, hash) → call Store with WriteContext → emit write_audit → return typed output
Tests use the in-memory SQLite store via runtime.Open (same pattern as tests/context and tests/project)
All orchestrator methods take ctx context.Context as first param

orchestrators.name: SessionStart
orchestrators.purpose: Open a session for an operator; set the active project; return sessionID + meta

orchestrators.name: SessionClose
orchestrators.purpose: Close a session; return summary of activity (counts of writes, runs, items)

orchestrators.name: ResearchTopic
orchestrators.purpose: Research pipeline: call backends, persist run + items, return ranked results
tests: happy path with mock backend returning 3 items

orchestrators.name: RecallContext
orchestrators.purpose: Wrap Store.Recall with economy compression; return ranked items within token budget

orchestrators.name: Judge
orchestrators.purpose: LLM-as-judge wrapper; persist SDDEvaluation; return verdict + confidence
tests: happy path returns verdict + saves SDDEval
canary check rejects content with canary token
low confidence (<0.5) still saves the verdict (no auto-reject)
